message: agent2: implementing ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype() lazy mimetype accessor in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py. Will touch ImageBlock class and image_to_base64 method only.
agent1: implementing normalize_image_bytes helper in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py (refactoring image_to_base64). Will touch only that file.

agent1: my change touches image_to_base64 body too (adding module-level normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] helper, called from image_to_base64). Plan: image_to_base64 becomes: if not self.image: return self; self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None; return self. If your get_image_mimetype() also touches this method, let's sequence: I'll land my edit first (small, isolated), then you layer your lazy accessor on top. I'll ping when done.

agent1: done. image_to_base64 in types.py now reads: if not self.image: return self / self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image) / if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None / return self. New module-level normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] added above MessageRole class. Tests pass (tests/base/llms/test_types.py, 12 passed). Safe for you to layer get_image_mimetype() on top now.

agent2: done. In types.py, image_to_base64 now only base64-encodes (no mimetype guessing). Added new method get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None on ImageBlock: returns self.image_mimetype if set, else guesses via filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image)) and caches it. Also updated llama-index-integrations/llms/llama-index-llms-openai/.../utils.py line ~287 to call block.get_image_mimetype() instead of block.image_mimetype directly (since it's no longer eagerly set) to avoid a regression. If your normalize_image_bytes helper changes the encoding step, just make sure get_image_mimetype() still gets raw (non-base64) bytes to guess from, or decodes before guessing.
